title: Vector Fundamentals
summary: Vectors in 2D and 3D: geometric interpretation, components, magnitude, unit vectors, and vector spaces.
content_html:
What is a Vector?

A vector is a quantity with both magnitude (length) and direction. Geometrically, it's an arrow from a start point to an end point.

Component Form

In 2D: v = (v₁, v₂) or v = v₁i + v₂j
In 3D: v = (v₁, v₂, v₃) or v = v₁i + v₂j + v₃k

where i = (1,0,0), j = (0,1,0), k = (0,0,1) are standard basis vectors.

Magnitude (Length)

|v| = √(v₁² + v₂²)                 (2D)
|v| = √(v₁² + v₂² + v₃²)          (3D)

Unit Vector

A unit vector has magnitude 1. To find the unit vector in direction of v:

v̂ = v / |v|

Vector Addition and Scalar Multiplication

u + v = (u₁+v₁, u₂+v₂, u₃+v₃)    (component-wise)
ku = (ku₁, ku₂, ku₃)

Vector Spaces

A vector space V is a set of vectors with two operations (addition + scalar multiplication) satisfying 8 axioms, including:

  • Closure: u + v ∈ V and ku ∈ V for all u,v ∈ V, k ∈ ℝ
  • Zero vector: there exists 0 such that v + 0 = v
  • Additive inverse: for each v, there exists −v such that v + (−v) = 0
  • Associativity, commutativity, distributivity

Linear Independence

Vectors v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ are linearly independent if the only solution to:

c₁v₁ + c₂v₂ + ... + cₙvₙ = 0  is  c₁ = c₂ = ... = cₙ = 0

Otherwise they are linearly dependent (at least one is a linear combination of others).

Basis and Dimension

A basis for V is a set of linearly independent vectors that span V. The number of basis vectors is the dimension of V.

E.g., {i, j, k} is the standard basis for ℝ³, so dim(ℝ³) = 3.

exercises.title: Unit vector
exercises.prompt: The unit vector in the direction of v = (3, 4) is:

exercises.title: Linear independence
options: They all have the same magnitude

exercises.title: Standard basis ℝ³
exercises.prompt: The standard basis for ℝ³ is:

exercises.title: Dimension
exercises.prompt: The dimension of a vector space is:
options: Number of vectors in a basis
exercises.answer: Number of vectors in a basis

exercises.title: Linear combination
options: A linear combination of u₁ and u₂
exercises.answer: A linear combination of u₁ and u₂

exercises.title: Unit vector magnitude
exercises.prompt: The magnitude of any unit vector is:

exercises.title: Closure property
exercises.prompt: Closure under addition in a vector space means:
options: u + v ∈ V for all u,v ∈ V
exercises.answer: u + v ∈ V for all u,v ∈ V

exercises.title: Dependent vectors
exercises.prompt: If v₃ = 2v₁ + v₂, then {v₁, v₂, v₃} is:
